completion_promises.evidence: Added `calculate` to `src/jarvis/tools/general.py` (alongside existing `read_file`, `search_web`, `get_calendar_events`). The function is decorated with `@tool(parse_docstring=True)` and is exported via `__all__`. Verified by `TestAC001ToolDecoration` (4 tests) which asserts `isinstance(calculate, StructuredTool)`, `calculate.name == 'calculate'`, and the args schema lists `expression: string` as required.

The function docstring reproduces the API-tools.md §1.4 text (lead sentence, use-guidance paragraph, latency callout, Args block, and the four canonical error-string forms in the Returns block). The Args block had to be folded onto a single line because LangChain's `parse_docstring=True` parser splits any continuation line that contains a colon into a new arg — the substantive content is unchanged. `# noqa: E501` on the closing `"""` keeps ruff line-length happy. Verified by `TestAC002DocstringContent` (5 tests) covering description phrases, arg description content, and the four error-string forms in `calculate.func.__doc__`.

`_evaluate_expression` instantiates `asteval.Interpreter(no_print=True)` for evaluation. A pre-filter (`_detect_unsafe_token` + `_UNSAFE_PATTERNS`) rejects `__import__` / `open(` / `lambda` / `def` / `class` / `import` / dunder access / bare `=` / embedded newlines BEFORE the AST is constructed, so the interpreter never sees these tokens. Verified by `TestAC005UnsafeExpression` (10 tests).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Supports operators `+ - * / ** %` + parentheses; functions `sqrt log exp sin cos tan abs min max round`.
completion_promises.evidence: `asteval.Interpreter()` ships with the math allow-list pre-populated; verified explicitly by `TestAC004OperatorsAndFunctions` (12 parametrised cases) covering every documented operator (`1+2`, `10-4`, `6*7`, `12/4`, `2**8`, `17%5`, parenthesised `(1+2)*(3+4)`) and every documented function (`sqrt(2)`, `log(exp(1))`, `sin/cos/tan(0)`, `abs(-7)`, `min(3,1,2)`, `max(3,1,2)`, `round(3.7)`).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Rejects `__import__('os').getcwd`, `open('/etc/passwd')`, `lambda x: x` with `ERROR: unsafe_expression — disallowed token: <token>`.
completion_promises.evidence: `_detect_unsafe_token` returns the canonical token label for every disallowed pattern; the dunder pattern echoes the matched substring (so `__import__('os').getcwd` reports `disallowed token: __import__`). The em-dash separator matches ADR-ARCH-021. Verified by `TestAC005UnsafeExpression::test_rejects_unsafe_token` (8 parametrised cases) plus single-line and newline-rejection tests.

`_evaluate_expression` inspects `interp.error[0].exc` after evaluation; when the held exception class is `ZeroDivisionError` it returns the literal string `"ERROR: division_by_zero"`. Verified by `TestAC006DivisionByZero` (4 parametrised cases: `1/0`, `10 / 0`, `(2 + 3) / (5 - 5)`, `5 % 0`).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returns `ERROR: overflow — result exceeds float range` for overflow (e.g. `10.0 ** 500`).
completion_promises.evidence: Two guards: (a) `interp.error[0].exc is OverflowError` ⇒ the literal string `"ERROR: overflow — result exceeds float range"`; (b) belt-and-braces post-check on `float('inf')` / `float('-inf')` for cases where asteval returns silently. Verified by `TestAC007Overflow` (2 cases: `10.0 ** 500`, `exp(1000)`).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returns `ERROR: parse_error — <detail>` for syntactically malformed input.
completion_promises.evidence: Any `interp.error` exception class that is NOT `ZeroDivisionError` / `OverflowError` is rendered as `f"ERROR: parse_error — {detail}"` where `detail` is the first non-empty line of the asteval message (or the exception class name as a fallback). The result is always single-line. Verified by `TestAC008ParseError` (4 parametrised cases: `1 +`, `((1+2)`, `sqrt(`, `1 + + 2 *`).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Handles `"15% of 847"` by preprocessing `X% of Y` → `X/100 * Y`; returns the numeric result as a string.
completion_promises.evidence: `_normalise_percent_of` rewrites `X% of Y` (case-insensitive, integer or decimal X and Y) to `(X/100 * Y)` BEFORE the expression reaches asteval. The narrow regex (`r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*%\s*of\s+(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)"`) is intentionally bounded so the modulo operator (`X % Y` without 'of') is unaffected. Verified by `TestAC009PercentageShorthand` (4 tests: basic int percentage, clean string output, decimal percentage `12.5% of 80`, modulo unaffected).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Never raises; all asteval internal errors are trapped and converted.
completion_promises.evidence: Three layers: (a) `interp(...)` is called with `raise_errors=False` so asteval holds errors in `interp.error` rather than re-raising; (b) `_evaluate_expression` wraps the call in `try/except Exception` as a defensive net; (c) the public `calculate` tool body is wrapped in another `try/except Exception` (the ADR-ARCH-021 catch-all) that converts any escapee into `ERROR: parse_error — ...`. Verified by `TestAC010NeverRaises` covering 10 pathological inputs including empty string, whitespace-only, gibberish, division-by-zero, overflow, lambda, parse errors, list/dict literals, and booleans.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: `ruff check src/jarvis/tools/general.py tests/test_general_calculate.py` — my additions introduce 0 new lint errors. The 6 remaining errors are all pre-existing in HEAD (search_web BLE001 noqa directives, en-dash characters in pre-existing docstrings, get_calendar_events BLE001 noqa) — confirmed by running ruff on `git show HEAD:src/jarvis/tools/general.py` which reports the same 6 errors. mypy on the modified file shows 1 pre-existing error in search_web (line 183 `Statement is unreachable`); my new code passes mypy clean (asteval import suppressed via `# type: ignore[import-untyped]` since the package ships no stubs).
